class.name: FmodGeometry
class.brief_description: Represents a geometry object for 3D audio occlusion calculations.
class.description: FmodGeometry is used to create geometric shapes that affect 3D audio propagation in the FMOD audio engine. It allows you to define polygons that act as occluders, blocking or attenuating sound based on their properties. Key features: - Create polygonal shapes that block sound paths between sources and listeners - Configure occlusion properties (direct and reverb path attenuation) - Support for double-sided polygons - Position, rotate, and scale geometry in 3D space - Serialize geometry data for efficient loading/saving When a sound source is behind geometry relative to the listener, FMOD calculates occlusion values that can be used to muffle the sound (direct occlusion) and reduce its reverb contribution (reverb occlusion). [b]Note:[/b] Geometry occlusion requires [constant FmodSystem.FMOD_INIT_FLAG_CHANNEL_LOWPASS] to be set during system initialization.

method.name: add_polygon
method.description: Adds a polygon to this geometry object. - [param direct_occlusion]: Occlusion factor for direct sound path (0.0 = no occlusion, 1.0 = full occlusion) - [param reverb_occlusion]: Occlusion factor for reverb path (0.0 = no occlusion, 1.0 = full occlusion) - [param double_sided]: If [code]true[/code], the polygon blocks sound from both sides. If [code]false[/code], only blocks from the front side. - [param num_vertices]: Number of vertices in the polygon (must be at least 3) - [param vertices]: Array of vertex positions defining the polygon Returns the index of the newly created polygon, or -1 on failure. [b]Note:[/b] The total number of polygons and vertices is limited by the max_polygons and max_vertices parameters specified when creating the geometry via [method FmodSystem.create_geometry].

method.name: geometry_is_null
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the geometry handle is null or invalid. A null geometry indicates that the object has not been properly initialized or has been released.

method.name: geometry_is_valid
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the geometry handle is valid and ready for use. A valid geometry can have polygons added and can be used for occlusion calculations.

method.name: get_active
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if this geometry object is currently active and being processed by the geometry engine. Inactive geometry does not contribute to occlusion calculations.

method.name: get_max_polygons
method.description: Returns a Dictionary containing the maximum capacity of this geometry object: - [code]max_polygons[/code]: Maximum number of polygons this geometry can hold - [code]max_vertices[/code]: Maximum number of vertices this geometry can hold These values are set when the geometry is created via [method FmodSystem.create_geometry].

method.name: get_num_polygons
method.description: Returns the number of polygons currently in this geometry object. Returns [code]-1[/code] if the geometry is invalid.

method.name: get_polygon_attributes
method.description: Returns the attributes of the specified polygon as a Dictionary: - [code]direct_occlusion[/code]: Direct sound occlusion factor (0.0-1.0) - [code]reverb_occlusion[/code]: Reverb occlusion factor (0.0-1.0) - [code]double_sided[/code]: Whether the polygon is double-sided Returns an empty Dictionary if the polygon index is invalid.

method.name: get_polygon_num_vertices
method.description: Returns the number of vertices in the specified polygon. Returns [code]-1[/code] if the polygon index is invalid.

method.name: get_polygon_vertex
method.description: Returns the position of a specific vertex in a polygon. - [param index]: The polygon index - [param vertex_index]: The vertex index within the polygon (0 to [method get_polygon_num_vertices] - 1) Returns [code]Vector3(0, 0, 0)[/code] if either index is invalid.

method.name: get_position
method.description: Returns the position of this geometry object in world space. The position is applied to all polygons in the geometry.

method.name: get_rotation
method.description: Returns the rotation of this geometry object in world space (in degrees). The rotation is applied to all polygons in the geometry.

method.name: get_save_size
method.description: Returns the size in bytes required to serialize this geometry object. Use this to allocate a buffer for [method save].

method.name: get_scale
method.description: Returns the scale of this geometry object. The scale is applied to all polygons in the geometry.

method.name: release
method.description: Releases this geometry object and frees its memory. After calling this method, the geometry becomes invalid and should not be used. Any polygons in the geometry are also destroyed.

method.name: save
method.description: Serializes this geometry object to a PackedByteArray. The saved data can be loaded later using [method FmodSystem.load_geometry]. This is useful for saving complex geometry to disk or transmitting it over a network. [b]Note:[/b] Use [method get_save_size] to determine the required buffer size beforehand.

method.name: set_active
method.description: Enables or disables this geometry object. When inactive ([code]false[/code]), the geometry does not contribute to occlusion calculations. Use this to toggle geometry on/off without rebuilding it.

method.name: set_polygon_attributes
method.description: Sets the attributes of an existing polygon. - [param index]: The polygon index - [param direct_occlusion]: Direct sound occlusion factor (0.0 = transparent, 1.0 = fully opaque) - [param reverb_occlusion]: Reverb occlusion factor (0.0 = transparent, 1.0 = fully opaque) - [param double_sided]: Whether the polygon blocks sound from both sides

method.name: set_polygon_vertex
method.description: Sets the position of a specific vertex in a polygon. - [param index]: The polygon index - [param vertex_index]: The vertex index within the polygon - [param vertex]: The new position of the vertex

method.name: set_position
method.description: Sets the position of this geometry object in world space. All polygons in the geometry are transformed by this position.

method.name: set_rotation
method.description: Sets the rotation of this geometry object in world space (in degrees). All polygons in the geometry are transformed by this rotation.

method.name: set_scale
method.description: Sets the scale of this geometry object. All polygons in the geometry are transformed by this scale.

members.member: Whether this geometry object is active and contributes to occlusion calculations.
The position of this geometry object in world space.
The rotation of this geometry object in world space (in degrees).
The scale of this geometry object.
